$schema_note: Draft rule table for UC-1 (NYC fee-cap audit). Schema is of our design and is documented inline in docs/research/uc1-rule-table.md §'JSON schema'. NOT wired into lib/packs/fees/ — that is slice F1. This file is a research artifact, human-readable, provider-agnostic.

meta.statute: NYC Administrative Code § 20-563.3 (Fee caps), Subchapter 36 (Third-Party Food Delivery Services)
meta.amending_law: Local Law 79 of 2025 (Council Int. No. 762-B of 2024)
meta.cap_base_term: purchase price of each online order
meta.source_primary: Enacted LL79/2025 certified PDF via intro.nyc/local-laws/2025-79 (pdftotext); Legistar Int 0762-2024 action history
meta.quarantine: All sources treated as untrusted data per Law 11; facts extracted only.
meta.coverage_disclaimer: Codifies enforceable constraints found in § 20-563.3 as amended. Does NOT claim to cover all edge cases or the full Subchapter 36. Penalty/restitution provisions live outside this section (UNVERIFIED).

definitions.text: fee for a service delivering food to customers within at least 1 mile of the establishment
fee for a service that (i) lets the establishment receive delivery/pickup orders on all of the platform's sites/apps and (ii) makes it searchable/discoverable by name in relevant results
fee for a service other than what basic-service/delivery/transaction fees cover
fee related to processing the online order
circumstances outside the platform's control, such as severe weather or natural disasters

rules.exception: may be satisfied instead by the monthly-average alternative NYC-563.3-a-2

rules.predicate: charges_delivery_fee => serves_customers_within_radius('>=1 mile of establishment') OR exigent_circumstances
rules.note: Structural obligation, not a fee-numeric check. Not machine-checkable from a statement alone.
No calendar-month averaging clause for transaction fees, and NO refund safe harbor (subd. e omits c).

rules.predicate: transaction_fee > 0.03 * order_purchase_price ALLOWED ONLY IF (i) line.amount == actual_processor_charge_for_order AND (ii) proof_available_to(department) AND proof_available_to(establishment)
rules.note: A transaction fee above 3% is unlawful UNLESS it is a documented dollar-for-dollar pass-through of the processor's charge with proof available on request.

rules.predicate: line.category IN {delivery_fee, basic_service_fee, transaction_fee, enhanced_service_fee} ; any other charged fee is unlawful
rules.note: Any fee that cannot be classified into the four permitted categories is per se unlawful. Catches disguised/relabeled/bundled charges and charges dressed as 'promotion deductions' when they are actually fees.
An enhanced service fee is permitted ONLY for a platform that also offers (and charges a basic service fee for) the basic service. Enhanced-without-basic is disallowed.

rules.predicate: over_cap_amount(subd in {a,b,d}) is NOT a violation IF refunded within 30 calendar days of the final day of the month in which the charge occurred
rules.note: Audit MUST NOT flag an over-cap month (a/b/d) as a violation until this 30-day window closes. The safe harbor does NOT extend to transaction-fee over-cap (subd. c). A 'refund' mislabeled or netted against other charges does not satisfy the safe harbor.
A fee change effective sooner than 30 days after notice (or a mid-month relabel not preceded by notice) is a violation. Supports cross-month relabeling detection.

rules.predicate: every fee/commission/charge under any agreement MUST be disclosed clear-and-conspicuous
rules.note: Undisclosed or opaquely-bundled charges violate the disclosure duty; enables bundling detection (an amount with no disclosed line = defect).

rules.predicate: each month the platform MUST provide an itemized list of each transaction including any fees charged, for the preceding month
rules.note: Defines the audit's minimum input contract: a monthly per-transaction itemized statement. A single bundled line covering multiple fee types violates itemization and defeats classification.
Context: value the basic service fee must deliver. Not statement-auditable numerically.

rules.predicate: commissioner report due <= 2026-09-30 covering fee-cap effects (factors 1-8 incl. penalties imposed, restitution recovered)
rules.note: Context only; confirms restitution/penalty enforcement exists under the subchapter (amounts UNVERIFIED here).

open_gaps.gap: 'purchase price of each online order' base not defined (tax/tip/discount inclusion)
open_gaps.impact: processing-fee base inflation + all cap checks

open_gaps.gap: penalty amounts / enforcement section outside § 20-563.3 not extracted
pre-LL79 (LL103/2021) verbatim text for statement periods straddling 2025-06-30
live amlegal-codified text not reconciled (403, re-attempted 2026-07-15); no later amendment found as of 2026-07-15
